notes:
Giật mình = to be suddenly startled or jump in surprise. It’s that quick body reaction when something unexpected happens.

It’s an intransitive verb, so you usually say someone giật mình because of something. Very often followed by a reason with khi, vì, or a sudden sound/event.  

Close words: hoảng hốt (more panicked), bất ngờ (just “surprised,” less physical). Giật mình often includes a small physical reaction (a jump, a quick movement).

definitions.en: To be startled or surprised suddenly.
definitions.es: Ser sobresaltado o sorprendido de repente.
definitions.vi: Bị giật mình hoặc ngạc nhiên một cách đột ngột.

example.es: Me sobresalté cuando escuché el ruido fuerte.
example.en: I was startled when I heard the loud noise.
example.vi: Tôi giật mình khi nghe tiếng động lớn.

exampleSentences: Em giật mình khi nghe tiếng động lớn ngoài cửa.
Anh thường giật mình mỗi khi ai đó gọi bất ngờ từ phía sau.
Chị ấy giật mình vì điện thoại rung trong lúc đang ngủ.

translation.en: I was startled when I heard the noise.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I was startled when I heard a loud noise outside the door.
He is often startled whenever someone calls him unexpectedly from behind.
She was startled because her phone vibrated while she was sleeping.
